updates.tweet_text:
For the first time in weeks today bears took control in #ES_F and shorts triggered on the failure of the multi-week 7473 shelf. We sold down to 7422 support, spent an hour there, trapped it briefly, then back up

7447-53 reclaim takes us to the 7473 backtest. Bottoms in if clears https://t.co/BYQhtkhA8v
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7473, 7422, 7447-53→7450) are morning-plan levels or observations of price action; no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Slow day after the initial volatility burst. 7473 failure was the first short trigger in weeks, giving full control to bears. Bears retain it until 7473 recovers. 7447-53 reclaims take us there. Newsletter out soon with triggers
updates.notes: 7473 and 7447-53 are morning-plan levels or reaffirmations; 'failure' and 'reclaims' are observations/trade management, not new actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text:
Yesterday we finally had a bears control day in #ES_F. 7473 multi-week shelf failure triggered down to 7422 support which we swept and recovered. 7447-53 then had to reclaim to see 7473 backtest, 5 points shy now

Plan today: 7447, 7438=supports. Keeps 7472 live, 7506 above there https://t.co/nueiMiGSQO
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7447, 7438, 7474/7472≈7474, 7506) are reaffirmations of morning plan levels; 7422/7473 are observations of prior price action, not new actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text: @KevinJagiah That's how it is all on bears control days. My whole edge is based on this concept, ES almost never sells in straight lines. Its why "bears" won't typically make money even in downtrends because shorts are very fast and short lived then we squeeze. Nearly impossible to make money
updates.notes: Conversational reply explaining market structure/trading philosophy. No price levels named, no actionable entries, targets, or triggers mentioned.

updates.tweet_text:
Volatility dropped again in #ES_F. 7473 remains the bull/bear line with bears in control below and bulls resuming control above. 7447-53 was key yesterday and we've been here for 12+ hours.

7447, 7438 remain supports to keep 7473, 7506+ live. Sell to newsletter entries <38 https://t.co/N0qt9tAfZv
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7473, 7447, 7438, 7506) are reaffirmations of morning-plan levels or prior references; no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @OnTheHooch There's no timeline on it as I've always said, the longest I've seen was about 2 years but usually its within in a month or two. Probably 3-4 weeks average
updates.notes: Reply tweet discussing timelines with no price levels mentioned at all.

updates.tweet_text: @alex_sidarenka We broke down a big support shelf causing a selloff, so bulls don't control again until that shelf recovers to invalidate it. It doesn't mean we cant bounce (the strongest bounces occur when bears control) but just don't expect them to last
updates.notes: No specific price levels named; purely conceptual commentary about support shelf breakdowns and bear-controlled bounces with no actionable prices.
